channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DNC gave ex-Harris booster 'no choice' but to leave, she says, as Dems cry turncoat
item.description: Lindy Li, a former Democratic fundraiser, tells Fox News Digital that she "didn't plan" on leaving the party amid questions surrounding her exit after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump backs idea to send some DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: President Donald Trump says he likes the idea to give some of the savings from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he's considering buying used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering buying used Boeing aircraft — perhaps from an overseas seller — to use as Air Force One when he’s aboard, as he fumes over the U.S. plane-maker’s delays in producing two specially modified ones for presidential use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs order aimed at ending federal benefits for some immigrants
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed an order aimed at ending federal benefits for people in the country illegally, the White House said Wednesday, his latest in a blizzard of moves to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats look to enshrine right to IVF in state law
item.description: Nevada Democrats introduced a proposal Wednesday to create a state law protecting the right to in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy war of words heats up even as US looks to wind down war in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy accused President Donald Trump of perpetuating Russian “disinformation" on Wednesday, while Trump clapped back and labeled Zelenskyy a “dictator" who has failed his country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP budget bill back on track hours after Trump threw it into turmoil
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Back on track, Senate Republicans pushed ahead Wednesday with their $340 billion budget bill focused on funding the White House's mass deportations and border security agenda after Vice President JD Vance gave a green light to proceed despite a morning dust-up caused by President D
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge homes in on DOGE staffer duties in privacy lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday looked to pinpoint the exact duties of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers during a hearing over whether to cut off their unfettered access to troves of sensitive personal data. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin directs federal agencies to end DEI considerations in govt contracts as DOGE's impact spreads
item.description: The Trump administration told federal contractors this week to stop considering DEI when handing out government contracts, in an effort to follow through on Trump's Day One executive order
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 groups to watch as House GOP eyes vote on advancing Trump agenda
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing a vote next week on their budget resolution to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda, an effort that increased in urgency after Trump endorsed the lower chamber’s strategy over the Senate’s blueprint.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department makes 5 drug cartels terrorist organization
item.description: Tren de Aragua, MS-13 and the Sinaloa and Jalisco cartels are among those named terrorists by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks greater control of independent regulators with his new executive order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is moving to give the White House direct control of independent federal regulators such as the Securities and Exchange Commission, the Federal Trade Commission and the Federal Communications Commission.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jan. 6 pardon recipients argue it stretches to other crimes: report
item.description: Many defendants are arguing that crimes that were found during the riot investigations should be pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Native American activist Leonard Peltier released from prison
item.description: Leonard Peltier, a Native American rights activist, was released from a federal prison in Florida after former President Joe Biden commuted his sentence in January.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Bird flu response personnel caught in federal workforce cuts
item.description: The firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans open to DOGE access to IRS but urge guardrails
item.description: Senate Republicans on Tuesday said they are open to the idea of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) accessing the IRS’s sensitive taxpayer information, as long as there are guardrails in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Judge to weigh arguments for dropping NYC Mayor Eric Adams' charges
item.description: Adams pleaded not guilty in September to allegations that he accepted at least $100K in illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Fentanyl on coroners' radar long before Nevada lawmakers took notice
item.description: The coroner's office knows what's happening in the community long before lawmakers do. That became clear during a discussion of deaths on Nevada's roads in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: For a St. Cloud family, Trump’s pause of refugee admissions brings worry for Afghan relatives
item.description: One of President Trump’s first actions after taking office was an executive order indefinitely suspending the U.S. refugee resettlement program. A family living in St. Cloud who fled Afghanistan a year ago worries about relatives left behind.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Senator Brian Jones to propose bill that would reform sanctuary state practices
item.description: Senator Brian Jones is looking to propose a bill that would shake up current legislation that shapes California as a sanctuary state.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: Health agencies lose staff members in key areas as Trump firings set in
item.description: As the dust settles from the first wave of firings at health agencies, here's how many people got cut and the impact of the roles that were lost.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
